question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Micrometer.observation(observationRegistry)` tap during a network partition to cause metric cardinality explosion, where each failed poll attempt creates a new observation span that is never closed, exhausting the metrics registry memory?
